mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal disposable income and housing starts indicate robust consumer spending and construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns in >50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, industrial production above 105 reflects resilient manufacturing output that offsets rate hike headwinds, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of observations when INDPRO exceeds 105 during tightening periods, or the regime-conditional alpha decays to zero within 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index crossing above 200 indicates entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >58% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, or the signal predictive power vanishes after controlling for term premium movements.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling total nonfarm payrolls signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor weakness signal yields positive returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in a walk-forward validation framework.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects strong aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters when GDPC1 shows positive growth, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, industrial production crossing below 100 indicates persistent supply chain bottlenecks and weak factory utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >62% of instances when INDPRO drops below 100 during recovery phases, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark after transaction costs.
